dialogue.speaker: system
dialogue.text: ENTER IDENTIFICATION PROTOCOL.

dialogue.speaker: player
dialogue.text: I am 24 years old, and I finally did it.

dialogue.speaker: player
dialogue.text: I actually conquered the absolute mountain of stress, late nights, and soul-crushing prep that was the government tax department exams.

dialogue.speaker: player
dialogue.text: It was brutal. Honestly, it took pieces of my soul I didn't know I had to give. But standing here right now? The view from the top of this mountain is beautiful.

dialogue.speaker: player
dialogue.text: This is the beginning of my actual life. A life where I make my own money, where I am completely independent, and where I don't have to answer to anyone about my future.

dialogue.speaker: player
dialogue.text: My ultimate goal? I'm going to save up, buy a solitary apartment deep in the woods, and just feel that pure independence pumping through my blood.

dialogue.speaker: player
dialogue.text: No noise, no dependency. Just me and the peace I earned.

dialogue.speaker: player
dialogue.text: But to get the woods, I have to survive the concrete jungle first.

dialogue.speaker: system
dialogue.text: REGIONAL TAX COLLECTION OFFICE - SECTOR 7G.

dialogue.speaker: player
dialogue.text: I adjust my collar and step in. I’m expecting a sleek operation, but it smells like old paper, stale coffee, and bureaucratic despair.

dialogue.speaker: player
dialogue.text: Ceiling fans lazily slice through the warm air, doing absolutely nothing to cool the room.

dialogue.speaker: system
dialogue.text: SENIOR INSPECTOR VANCE: UNRESPONSIVE AT DESK.

dialogue.speaker: vance
dialogue.text: Ah. The new blood. You must be {playerName}.

dialogue.speaker: vance
dialogue.text: Enjoy the high of passing those exams while it lasts, kid. Because right now, you are at the absolute bottom of the food chain.

dialogue.speaker: vance
dialogue.text: You see this folder? These are the Ghosts. The chronic defaulters.

dialogue.speaker: vance
dialogue.text: People and businesses that haven't paid a dime in years. They ignore letters, they dodge phone calls, and they eat junior officers like you for breakfast.

dialogue.speaker: vance
dialogue.text: Your job is to go out there, be logical, be firm, and squeeze water from these stones. If you fail, you’ll be pushing papers in the basement archive until you retire. Understood?

choices.label: I understand perfectly. They haven't dealt with me yet.
Understood. I'll review their financials and close the net.
Yes, sir. I’m here to do a job. Point me to the first target.

dialogue.speaker: vance
dialogue.text: Big words for a 24-year-old. Let's see if your spine matches your mouth.

dialogue.speaker: vance
dialogue.text: Good. Brains over brawn. Let's see if that textbook logic holds up in the real world.

dialogue.speaker: vance
dialogue.text: Straight to the point. I can appreciate that. Try to keep that focus.

dialogue.speaker: vance
dialogue.text: Your first target is a local auto-repair shop owner named 'Greasy' Pete. Owes five years of back taxes.

dialogue.speaker: vance
dialogue.text: He claims his business is failing, but his social media shows him buying a new jet ski every summer. He’s loud, he’s aggressive, and he hates us.

dialogue.speaker: vance
dialogue.text: Take a department vehicle. Don't let him intimidate you. Remember, you have the full weight of the tax code behind you.

dialogue.speaker: player
dialogue.text: I pick up the heavy file. The paper is rough against my fingers. Greasy Pete.

dialogue.speaker: player
dialogue.text: I step out of the office and into the glaring sunlight, gripping the folder tight. I need this win. I need that apartment in the woods.

dialogue.speaker: player
dialogue.text: I stare at the shop sign: Pete’s Premium Auto. Premium? My foot.

dialogue.speaker: player
dialogue.text: I’ve spent the last hour cross-referencing his filings. The idiot claims he’s 'operating at a net loss' for the fifth year in a row.

dialogue.speaker: player
dialogue.text: I step into the garage. The smell of burnt oil and cigarette smoke is thick.

dialogue.speaker: player
dialogue.text: Peter 'Greasy' Pete? My name is {playerName}, Junior Recovery Officer.

dialogue.speaker: pete
dialogue.text: Another government parasite? What the hell are you doing here, kid? Get lost before I lose my temper.

dialogue.speaker: player
dialogue.text: In the red? Interesting. Then I assume that $150,000 luxury sports car sitting in the back garage is a donation?

dialogue.speaker: pete
dialogue.text: Listen to me, you government parasite! That car has nothing to do with you. Get off my property before I teach you a lesson.

dialogue.speaker: system
dialogue.text: TENSION LEVEL: CRITICAL. SUBJECT IS ARMED WITH CROWBAR.

choices.label: Look, Pete. I’m here to help you avoid a jail cell. Let's talk.
Pay me now and keep your shop, or lose your life's work. Choice is yours.
[DRAW SERVICE WEAPON] Pay the taxes, or go to Hell. Write the check.

dialogue.speaker: system
dialogue.text: SCENE UNDER DEVELOPMENT. CHAPTER 1 PART 2 CONTINUES...

dialogue.speaker: system
dialogue.text: SCENE UNDER DEVELOPMENT. CHAPTER 1 PART 2 CONTINUES...

dialogue.speaker: system
dialogue.text: SCENE UNDER DEVELOPMENT. CHAPTER 1 PART 2 CONTINUES...

dialogue.speaker: system
dialogue.text: CHAPTER 1: BLOOD, SWEAT, AND TAXES - COMPLETED.
